2606.31976.title: TreeAgent: A Generalizable Multi-Agent Framework for Automated Bias Labeling in Forestry via Compiled Expert Rules and Vision-Language Models
2606.31976.paper_summary: Human-labeled data are widely used as reference annotations in ML, despite known variability across annotators in many expert-driven domains. In addition, expert annotation is slow, inconsistent, and remains a major bottleneck for scaling tasks like tree height bias classification in forestry remote sensing. We propose a multi-agent system (MAS) that orchestrates expert decision trees with Vision-Language Models (VLMs), treating the decision tree as a structural prior while VLMs perform localized semantic perception at individual nodes, with multi-agent voting to mitigate VLM stochasticity. We formalize a Decoupled Declarative Decision (D3) Framework that enables zero-modification generalization across diverse expert-defined decision structures. On a tree bias classification testbed, our framework outperforms supervised ML baselines and reduces the amount of expert labeling effort required. These results suggest that agentic orchestration of VLMs with expert priors can reproduce expert-defined labeling procedures at substantially lower annotation cost while maintaining interpretability.
2606.31976.paper_summary_zh: 人類標記的數據在機器學習中被廣泛用作參考註釋，儘管在許多專家驅動的領域中，標註者之間存在已知的變異性。<lb/>此外，專家標註的速度慢且不一致，並且仍然是擴展像林業遙感中的樹高偏差分類等任務的一個主要瓶頸。<lb/>我們提出了一個多代理系統（MAS），該系統協調專家決策樹與視覺-語言模型（VLMs），將決策樹視為結構性先驗，而VLMs則在各個節點執行局部語義感知，並通過多代理投票來減輕VLM的隨機性。<lb/>我們形式化了一個解耦的聲明式決策（D3）框架，該框架使得在多樣化的專家定義決策結構中實現零修改的泛化成為可能。<lb/>在樹偏差分類的測試平台上，我們的框架超越了監督式機器學習基準，並減少了所需的專家標註工作量。<lb/>這些結果表明，將VLM與專家先驗進行代理協調可以以顯著較低的註釋成本重現專家定義的標註程序，同時保持可解釋性。
2606.31976.author: Shiyi Chen et.al.
2606.31976.authors: Shiyi Chen, Nicholas Saban, Collin Hargreaves, Huiqi Wang
